subject.title: Mathematics Applications & Interpretation (AI)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics: Applications and Interpretation** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **real-world applications of mathematics, statistical analysis, mathematical modeling, and data interpretation**, with a strong focus on **technology use, practical problem-solving, and interpreting mathematical results in context**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | External examination (calculator permitted) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 2** | External examination (technology required) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | External examination (technology required) | N/A | 1h | HL: 20% |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal assessment | 30h | 30h | 20% |

strengths: Logical narrative with clear introduction, body, and conclusion
Wellâformatted table of contents and numbered sections aid navigation
Coherent sequencing of data collection, modelling, and testing
weaknesses: Occasional digressions and repeated justifications interrupt flow
Some figures unlabeled or duplicated, hindering smooth reading
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration is coherent and logically developed with a recognizable structure and clear transitions, meriting band 3. However, digressions, repeated explanations, and occasional misplacement or duplication of figures prevent it reaching the top band.

strengths: Consistent use of statistical notation (p, ÏÂ², Î±, RMSE) in most cases
Effective use of multiple representations (tables, graphs, sine fits, chiâsquare summaries)
Clear matching of text explanations to visuals
weaknesses: Inconsistent overline notation and decimal formatting for pâvalues
Occasional unexplained changes in notation (df formula)
Lack of explicit linking between coefficients and model equations
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Mathematical language and notation are mostly appropriate (band 3), and representations are effective. Minor inconsistencies and lapses in clarity prevent a topâband score.

strengths: Clear personal narrative driving the choice of data and questions
Evidence of independent thinking in selecting trial papers and combining regression with ÏÂ² analysis
Initiative shown by compiling multiâyear SPM data and devising a twoâpronged statistical approach
weaknesses: Mathematical execution sometimes overshadows the narrative rather than integrating with it
Procedures largely follow standard textbook methods
Limited extension beyond expected classroom techniques
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work shows significant personal engagement with clear independent thinking and initiative, placing it solidly in band 2. It does not demonstrate the outstanding creativity or depth of approach required for band 3.

strengths: Meaningful reflection on model limitations (sample size, curriculum changes)
Discussion of how findings inform future candidates links to understanding
Acknowledgement of deviations and critique of the sinusoidal fit
weaknesses: Depth of analysis is sometimes superficial, lacking quantification of effects
Critical interrogation of statistical assumptions (independence, df choice) is limited
Reflections remain general rather than deeply analytic
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Reflection is present and shows meaningful analysis (band 2), but it lacks the depth and critical rigourâincluding exploration of alternative models or detailed sensitivity analysisâneeded to reach band 3.

strengths: Use of HLâlevel techniques (sinusoidal regression, RMSE, binomial expectation, ÏÂ²) relevant to the research aim
Correct presentation of sinusoidal model form and cogent justification for its use
Advanced application of mathematics, drawing substantive conclusions from results
weaknesses: Conceptual inaccuracies in degrees of freedom and binomialâmodel assumptions
Calculation errors in RMSE and probability computations
Uneven understanding when interpreting pâvalues and statistical parameters
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The mathematics is clearly at course level and used effectively (band 4), and much of it is correct and sophisticated. However, conceptual missteps and computational inaccuracies (band 3 in accuracy and understanding) mean the work does not demonstrate full rigour. A score of 5 reflects strong use of mathematics tempered by these errors.

strandSelections.reasoning: Selecting Malaysian trial papers, choosing sinusoidal regression and constructing a goodness-of-fit strategy shows the studentâs own thinking beyond classroom templates.  Nonetheless, most procedures (desmos fit, standard ÏÂ² test) follow textbook methods with limited extension or comparison, indicating âsignificantâ but not âoutstandingâ independent thinking.

The student demonstrates good understanding when explaining why sinusoidal models capture periodic behaviour and when interpreting p-values.  Misinterpretation of binomial assumptions and limited discussion of parameter meanings indicate understanding is uneven; it is solid in parts but not thorough throughout.

Core calculations (regression parameters, RMSE, ÏÂ² values) appear numerically plausible, but conceptual inaccuracies exist: the df formula is misstated, p is sometimes computed by dividing twice by 13, and the rationale for a binomial model for counts out of 13 is not fully justified.  These constitute âminorâ to moderate errors, so the work is âmostly correctâ rather than fully correct.

Most of the material is relevant to modelling examination topics, yet the report contains noticeable repetition (e.g. repeated explanation of why n = 13, multiple restatements of chi-square decision rules) and lengthy narrative anecdotes that do not advance the mathematics.  These non-essential passages reduce conciseness, placing the work in the âmostly relevantâ rather than âfully conciseâ range.

Variables, parameters and statistical symbols (p, ÏÂ², Î±, RMSE) are used correctly in most cases.  Sinusoidal model coefficients are written clearly and equations are typeset.  There are, however, inconsistencies such as unexplained overline notation for zero, and occasional misuse of boldface.  Overall, language is mostly appropriate but not flawless.

Explanations generally match accompanying visuals, and notation is applied consistently within sections, giving clear communication.  Minor lapses (e.g. changing df formula without justification, spelling âAGAINSTSâ) create some inconsistency, preventing the highest band.

Sinusoidal regression, RMSE, binomial expectation and ÏÂ² goodness-of-fit are all at HL AI course level and clearly relevant to the aim.  They are used purposefully to draw conclusions and are mostly appropriate, though not extended into higher-level techniques (e.g., parameter estimation uncertainty, alternative distributions).

The student employs several representations: raw-data tables, line graphs, sine-curve overlays, RMSE table, and chi-square summary tables.  These capture the mathematics effectively and allow comparison between observed and expected values.  The range is good but not especially sophisticated (no dynamic or interactive representations, limited use of residual plots), so the âeffectiveâ level is appropriate.

The student reflects on model limitations (small sample, curriculum changes, model choice) and interprets RMSE and p-values in context, indicating meaningful reflection.  Reflection is present throughout, but depth is sometimes superficial (e.g., stating ÏÂ² is non-parametric without linking to consequences) so it does not reach âsubstantial critical reflectionâ.

Gathering 26 separate state trial papers, compiling three years of actual SPM data, and deciding to test both regression and distributional assumptions demonstrates noticeable initiative.  Creativity is evident in combining RMSE with ÏÂ² to judge usefulness.  The work remains within fairly standard statistical tools, so the âsignificantâ band is the closest match.

The exploration follows a recognisable narrative: introductionâdata collectionâmodellingâstatistical testingâconclusion.  Tables, graphs and discussion appear in a logical order and earlier statements are usually revisited later, showing deliberate sequencing.  Occasional digressions (anecdotal teacher story, repeated justification of sample size) interrupt the flow, so the development is clear but not exemplary.

There is some critique of the sinusoidal fit, acknowledgement of deviations, and discussion of why different years yield different ÏÂ² results.  Analysis is meaningful yet not deep: alternative models or re-parameterisation are not explored, and statistical assumptions (independence, choice of df) are not interrogated rigorously.

The student articulates how the findings could help future candidates and how model shortcomings limit reliability, indicating a clear connection to personal mathematical understanding.  The enhancement of understanding is limited to general statements rather than detailed insight, placing it in the second band.

Headings, a table of contents, numbered sections and sub-sections make the structure easy to follow.  Introduction, body and conclusion are all present and distinct.  Transitions between the regression investigation and the goodness-of-fit investigation are explicit.  However, some tables sit several pages away from reference in the text and a handful of figures are unlabeled or duplicated, preventing a âsmoothâ or âseamlessâ experience.

The student frames the study around personal experience with a teacher and exam preparation, and maintains that narrative throughout.  This personal context clearly drives the choice of data and questions, satisfying a âclear personal approachâ, though the mathematical execution sometimes overshadows the narrative rather than integrating with it fully.

annotations.comment: Assuming a binomial model requires independence and constant p; provide justification for these assumptions given topic selection across states.
The title page is visually clear and immediately conveys the focus of the exploration with bold headings and session details.
Consider refining the research question here to specify exactly which topics and time frame will be analyzed, enhancing clarity of purpose.
The table of contents is well formatted with clear section headings and corresponding page numbers, aiding navigation.
Page references in the contents table appear duplicated (âp-value Trial 2021â twice) and could confuse readersâensure each entry is distinct and correctly aligned.
The introductionâs narrative is highly engaging, demonstrating clear personal engagement and motivation behind the investigation.
The research aim is stated but lacks a succinct research question. Condense narrative and clearly pose the question guiding the modeling and testing.
The aim and scope are clearly reiterated here with structure, setting up the regression and goodness-of-fit tests in context.
Overline notation (e.g., \overline{4}) appears without explanation. Define any nonstandard symbols in a legend or footnote.
The description of Table 1 repeats earlier statements. A concise summary with only key observations would improve relevance and reduce redundancy.
The sample-size justification is repeated with unnecessary detail on meeting the 30-data rule; condense to maintain conciseness.
Incorporating 2015â2022 SPM data demonstrates thorough use of relevant mathematics to underpin regression analysis.
Figure 3âs caption doesnât note the missing 2020 data point; annotate the gap for clearer interpretation.
The force-vs-year graph lacks axis labels and units, hindering reader interpretation of scales and values.
The textual description of the wave graph effectively matches the figure, demonstrating clear communication of trends.
The third figure uses the same internal ID as a previous figure, risking confusion; assign unique labels to avoid duplication.
Consider adding residual plots to support the choice of sinusoidal regression and highlight model fit quantitatively.
The justification for using sinusoidal regression is cogent and shows understanding of periodic patterns.
Clarify how converting calendar years to sequential x-values (1â2015, etc.) preserves unit consistency and affects parameter interpretation.
The general sinusoidal form is correctly presented, clearly laying out the model structure.
The link between the coefficients A,B,C,D and the fitted equations yâ,yâ,yâ is not made explicit; define each parameterâs interpretation.
Include all residuals squared and their sum explicitly in Table 4âs cells to allow readers to verify the RMSE computation.
The RMSE is calculated as â(5.9155/7), yet the numerical value of Î£EÂ² differs from the tableâreconcile these values for accuracy.
The computation p = (Î£observed)/13 Ã· 13 applies the divisor twice, leading to an incorrect probability; revise to p = Î£observed / (nÂ·k) appropriately.
The degrees of freedom formula df = n â 2 is incorrect for goodness-of-fit; the standard is df = (number of categories â 1) â parameters estimated.
The evaluation of chi-square test limitations and model assumptions shows critical reflection but could be deepened by quantifying effect sizes.
Table 6 lacks a final row explicitly summing ÏÂ² contributions; add this to enhance transparency of the test statistic.
Chi-square results are presented clearly with test statistic and p-value, showing consistent use of notation.
In Table 7 the p-value is shown as 0.99512 but described elsewhere as 0.995; maintain consistent decimal formatting throughout.
Unify p-value decimal places in text and summary tables to improve clarity of statistical decision reporting.
The discussion on syllabus changes and variability in topic probabilities illustrates thoughtful reflection on external factors influencing results.
The personal reflection on challenges and learning highlights genuine engagement and growth through the exploration.
The limitation concerning omitted topic weightings is insightful; consider incorporating weighting factors or sensitivity analysis in future work.
